Notion Alternatives for Sales Teams and CRM — Complete Guide [2026]

AI AI Tools Idea · May 25, 2026 · Updated May 25, 2026
— min read
Affiliate disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our links — at no extra cost to you. Learn more →
🚀 AI Platform

As a small business owner, you’re likely struggling to find the perfect tool to manage your sales team’s workflow and customer relationships, and you’re considering Notion alternatives for sales teams and CRM. Notion is an excellent all-in-one workspace that offers a wide range of features, including notes, tasks, databases, and pages. However, it may not be the best fit for every sales team, especially those with specific CRM needs. If you’re looking for a more tailored solution, you’re in the right place.

Notion is a powerful tool that offers a lot of flexibility and customization options. However, its pricing can be a bit steep for small businesses, with the Team plan starting at $8 per user per month, and the Enterprise plan starting at $20 per user per month, as of 2026. Try Notion free to see if it’s the right fit for your sales team.

Here’s a comparison table of Notion and some of its top alternatives for sales teams and CRM:

ToolPricingFeatures
Notion$8-$20 per user per monthNotes, tasks, databases, pages
HubSpot CRMFree-$1,200 per monthContact management, sales pipeline, marketing automation
Pipedrive$15-$59 per user per monthSales pipeline, contact management, reporting
Copper$19-$49 per user per monthSales pipeline, contact management, Google Drive integration
Airtable$12-$24 per user per monthDatabases, tables, forms, views

Pro tip: When choosing a Notion alternative, consider the specific needs of your sales team. If you need a robust CRM with advanced features, HubSpot or Copper might be a better fit. If you’re looking for a more affordable option with a focus on sales pipeline management, Pipedrive or Airtable could be the way to go.

People switch from Notion to other tools for various reasons, including:

  • Lack of CRM-specific features: Notion is a general-purpose workspace that can be used for a wide range of tasks, but it may not offer the advanced CRM features that sales teams need.
  • Pricing: As mentioned earlier, Notion’s pricing can be a bit steep for small businesses, especially those with large sales teams.
  • Complexity: Notion can be overwhelming for some users, especially those who are not tech-savvy. Other tools may offer a more streamlined and user-friendly experience.

Let’s take a closer look at some of the top Notion alternatives for sales teams and CRM:

Free Options

  • HubSpot CRM: HubSpot offers a free CRM plan that includes contact management, sales pipeline, and marketing automation features. It’s a great option for small businesses that want to get started with CRM without breaking the bank.
  • Airtable: Airtable offers a free plan that includes databases, tables, forms, and views. It’s a great option for small businesses that want to manage their sales pipeline and customer relationships in a flexible and customizable way.

Paid Options

  • Pipedrive: Pipedrive is a sales-focused CRM that offers a range of features, including sales pipeline, contact management, and reporting. It starts at $15 per user per month and offers a 14-day free trial.
  • Copper: Copper is a Google-recommended CRM that offers a range of features, including sales pipeline, contact management, and Google Drive integration. It starts at $19 per user per month and offers a 14-day free trial.
  • Try Notion’s paid plans for more advanced features and support.

Pro tip: When choosing a paid Notion alternative, consider the cost per user and the features that are included. Make sure to calculate the total cost of ownership and compare it to the benefits that the tool will bring to your sales team.

Another pro tip: Don’t be afraid to try out multiple tools before committing to one. Most Notion alternatives offer free trials or demos, so take advantage of these to see which tool is the best fit for your sales team.

In addition to the tools mentioned above, there are many other Notion alternatives for sales teams and CRM. Some other options to consider include:

  • Salesforce: A robust CRM that offers a range of features, including sales pipeline, contact management, and marketing automation.
  • Zoho CRM: A cloud-based CRM that offers a range of features, including sales pipeline, contact management, and reporting.
  • Freshsales: A sales-focused CRM that offers a range of features, including sales pipeline, contact management, and reporting.

When choosing a Notion alternative, it’s essential to consider the specific needs of your sales team. Think about the features that are must-haves, the budget you have available, and the level of complexity that your team can handle. By doing your research and trying out different tools, you can find the perfect Notion alternative for your sales team and CRM needs.

Learn more about Notion’s features and pricing to see if it’s the right fit for your business.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are the best Notion alternatives for sales teams and CRM?

The best Notion alternatives for sales teams and CRM include HubSpot CRM, Pipedrive, Copper, and Airtable. These tools offer a range of features, including sales pipeline, contact management, and reporting, and are designed specifically for sales teams. When choosing a Notion alternative, consider the specific needs of your sales team and the features that are must-haves.

How much do Notion alternatives cost?

The cost of Notion alternatives varies widely, depending on the tool and the features that are included. Some tools, like HubSpot CRM, offer free plans, while others, like Pipedrive and Copper, start at $15-$20 per user per month. It’s essential to calculate the total cost of ownership and compare it to the benefits that the tool will bring to your sales team.

What features should I look for in a Notion alternative?

When choosing a Notion alternative, look for features that are specific to sales teams and CRM, such as sales pipeline, contact management, and reporting. Consider the level of complexity that your team can handle and the budget you have available. It’s also essential to think about the integration with other tools and the level of customer support that is offered.

Can I use Notion for sales teams and CRM?

Yes, Notion can be used for sales teams and CRM, but it may not be the best fit for every team. Notion is a general-purpose workspace that can be used for a wide range of tasks, but it may not offer the advanced CRM features that sales teams need. If you’re looking for a more tailored solution, consider trying out a Notion alternative that is specifically designed for sales teams and CRM.
